business.headline: Tata Electronics hit by cyberattack; 2 lakh files linked to Apple, Tesla leakedThe breach is the latest setback for Apple's supply chain in India, where Tata faces scrutiny over alleged contamination of farmlands near one of its iPhone parts plants, Reuters reported.

India's economy is booming. Both the rebels and the leaders refrained from direct attacks on one another. Is India witnessing a new, sugar-coated style of political defection?

IITs ask students not to reveal JEE rank for campus placements. There's a quota angleThe All IITs Placement Committee (AIPC) has directed students across all 23 IITs to remove JEE ranks, GATE scores, percentiles and similar entrance-exam credentials from resumes used for campus placements and internships. The announcement has sparked an intense debate.

Live: Trump says Iran will accept nuclear weapon inspections despite Tehran's pushbackUS-Iran Peace Deal News Live Updates: US-Iran diplomacy gained momentum after a marathon round of talks in Switzerland produced what mediators Pakistan and Qatar described as a roadmap for a final agreement within 60 days. US Vice President JD Vance said Tehran had agreed to allow nuclear inspectors into the country and work on mechanisms covering frozen Iranian assets, regional ceasefires and the Strait of Hormuz. Vance said discussions on nuclear inspections could begin as early as this week, while technical teams will remain engaged even after Iran's top negotiators returned to Tehran following 18 hours of intensive negotiations. The talks also established a communication channel to help ensure safe commercial shipping through Strait of Hormuz and a mechanism aimed at preventing renewed fighting in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. Despite the diplomatic progress, tensions remained visible. Iran had briefly closed the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, accusing the United States of failing to uphold commitments to halt fighting in Lebanon. However, mediators said discussions were held in a constructive atmosphere and yielded encouraging progress. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian is set to visit Pakistan on Tuesday in a trip expected to focus on bilateral cooperation and follow-up discussions on Islamabad's mediation efforts. Meanwhile, a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon largely held, marking the longest lull in violence in three months of conflict. Lebanese officials reported near-total adherence to the truce despite isolated incidents, while Israeli leaders insisted any final agreement must ensure Iran cannot use future financial relief to support military activities or regional proxy groups. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

UAE seeks to buy BrahMos, Akashteer to boost post-Middle East war defenceAs the UAE moves to bolster its defence capabilities following the Iran war, it has initiated early-stage talks with India to acquire the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile and the Akashteer air defence system.

Why are homeowners turning to thermocol wall technology for cooling?As temperatures continue to rise, EPS wall technology is drawing interest as a cooling solution for homes. Made with thermocol-based panels, the construction method aims to reduce heat transfer, improve insulation and lower energy use.

sports.headline: England name squad for T20I series against India, uncapped all-rounder called up England have named a 17-player squad for the T20I series against India, with James Coles receiving his first call-up.
